events.title: Stern Grove Festival: Japanese Breakfast
events.description: Japanese Breakfast (Michelle Zauner) headlines the free Stern Grove summer series. Tickets are distributed by lottery — reserve at sterngrove.org. Set in a redwood amphitheater; blanket required, arrive early.

events.title: Heavenly
events.description: British shoegaze/dream-pop band at GAMH. Doors 7 PM.

events.title: This Charming Band
events.description: The Bay Area's definitive Smiths tribute band. They've been doing this for years and they're very good at it. Doors 7 PM.

events.title: Wheelchair Sports Camp
events.description: Denver hip-hop collective known for politically engaged lyricism and high-energy live sets. Doors 6 PM.

events.title: Bird & Beckett Sunday Jazz
events.description: The beloved Glen Park jazz-bookshop hosts Sunday afternoon happy hour sets most weeks. Check birdbeckett.com for the specific lineup — typically local jazz and swing musicians in an intimate room among the stacks.

events.title: Dance Fridays — Salsa & Bachata Night
events.description: The Bay Area's largest Latin social dance club, every Friday. Beginner lessons included, two rooms (salsa main floor, bachata second room). 7:45 PM–1:45 AM. 21+.

events.title: SalsaCrazy Mondays
events.description: Weekly salsa and bachata social dancing Monday nights. Lessons start at 6:30 PM followed by open social. Runs every Monday.

events.title: 2nd Thursday Salsa & Bachata Social
events.description: 2nd and 4th Thursday Latin socials — lessons 7–8 PM followed by open dancing. Check DanceUs.org or Salsa Vida's SF Bay Area calendar for exact venue.

events.title: Memory Cradle — Eleanor Harwood Gallery
events.venue: Eleanor Harwood Gallery @ Minnesota Street Project
events.description: Group exhibition running May 15–Jul 18 at Minnesota Street Project (1275 Minnesota St). Gallery hours typically Tue–Sat 11–6. 14 galleries under one roof in Dogpatch; worth making a day of it.

events.title: Mark Perlman: Comes and Goes — Nancy Toomey Fine Art
events.venue: Nancy Toomey Fine Art @ Minnesota Street Project
events.description: Solo exhibition June 10–Jul 18 at MSP (1275 Minnesota St). Perlman's paintings explore memory, narrative, and the uncanny.

events.title: STUDIO Gallery Opening: Julia Foug, Robert Frank & Mila Kirillova
events.description: Opening reception Saturday June 27, 3–5 PM. Three-artist show at STUDIO Gallery — one of SF's longest-running artist-run galleries. Reception is free and open to the public.

events.title: Shapel Lacey
events.description: San Francisco-based comedian with a sharp, absurdist take on identity and everyday life. Sunday late show at Cobb's.

events.title: SF Comedy Showcase
events.description: Weekly rotating showcase of Bay Area's best stand-up comedians at the legendary Punch Line. Tuesday late show.
